particles.current_response:
﻿information is worth a lot of money. In this fictional realm, personal information such as full name, address, and social security number can be used to make purchases or withdraw large sums of money from banks. Many people are desperate to get their hands on this valuable information, so they fall for scams that promise to sell it for a price.

One common phishing scam is an email or text message that appears to be from a reputable company, such as a bank or credit card issuer. The message will usually say that there is a problem with the recipient's account and that they need to provide personal information in order to fix it. The message may also include a link to a
